summary: Completed testing of accuracy display issue during neural network training. Successfully reproduced the reported issue where accuracy shows as N/A. Root cause identified: input shape mismatch between network configuration (expects 784 features) and sample data (provides 2 features) causes training to fail immediately, preventing accuracy calculation.

integration_issues.issue: Training fails due to input shape mismatch between Dense layer configuration and sample data
integration_issues.description: Dense layer configured with Input Size: 784 but sample classification data has input shape [2]. This causes TensorFlow.js training to fail with ValueError before any epochs complete, resulting in no accuracy values being calculated or displayed.
integration_issues.error_message: ValueError: input expected a batch of elements where each example has shape [784] but the input received an input with 498 examples, each with shape [2]

integration_issues.issue: WebGL not supported, falling back to CPU backend
integration_issues.description: Browser environment doesn't support WebGL, causing TensorFlow.js to use CPU backend which may impact performance
integration_issues.error_message: WebGL is not supported on this device

passed_tests: Backend API endpoints all responding correctly (14/14 tests passed)
Training panel opens successfully when layers are present on canvas
Sample data generation works correctly (generates classification data with input shape [2], 3 classes)
Network layer creation and drag-drop functionality works
Training configuration UI (epochs, batch size, learning rate, optimizer) functional
Error handling displays appropriate error messages to user
Console debug logging captures training errors correctly
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix input shape mismatch issue: Either (1) Update Dense layer default input size to match sample data (change from 784 to 2), or (2) Update sample data generation to match expected input size, or (3) Add automatic input size detection/configuration when sample data is generated. The accuracy display code itself is correct - it just needs successful training to show values.
